channel.title: Cool TravelPal Guides
channel.description: Practical travel guides, credit-card rewards advice, and destination-experience guides from Cool TravelPal.

item.title: MSP Delta Sky Club G18 Review: Quiet and Work-Friendly
item.description: A firsthand review of MSP’s largest Delta Sky Club, including location, hours, seating, soundproof booths, food, bar, art, and 2026 access rules.

item.title: Six Days in Xi’an: A History-First Itinerary
item.description: A practical route through major museums, the Terracotta Army, pagodas, Beilin, the City Wall, archaeology, and an easier Xi’an North departure.

item.title: Alila Shanghai Review: Warm Staff, Weak Execution
item.description: A candid review of friendly service, failed room-service ordering, Shanghai breakfast, slow restaurant pacing, billing clarity, and Jing’an location.

item.title: Hyatt Regency Xi’an Review: Spacious, Good Value, Not Central
item.description: An independent review of a 100-square-meter Globalist suite upgrade, service, dated rooms, breakfast, dining, and the quiet Qujiang location.

item.title: Shaanxi History Museum Tickets & Crowd Strategy
item.description: The official reservation process, passport entry, paid exhibitions, current hours, highlights, and a calmer route through Xi’an’s most competitive museum.

item.title: Giant Wild Goose Pagoda Xi’an: Afternoon & Evening Guide
item.description: A practical afternoon-to-evening plan for Daci’en Temple, Xuanzang, tower access, photography, the fountain, and Grand Tang Mall.

item.title: Is Shaanxi Archaeology Museum Worth Visiting?
item.description: A firsthand guide to field methods, cultural chronology, Tang murals, conservation, reservations, passport entry, and transport.

item.title: Terracotta Army Xi’an Guide: Tickets, Route & Lishan Garden
item.description: A firsthand guide to the warrior pits, Lishan Garden, bronze chariots, tickets, transport, crowds, and accessibility.

item.title: Qianling Mausoleum: A Worthwhile Day Trip from Xi’an
item.description: A firsthand guide to transport, tickets, Wu Zetian’s Wordless Stele, the imperial stone avenue, and three excavated satellite tombs.

item.title: Is Xi’an Museum and Small Wild Goose Pagoda Worth Visiting?
item.description: An honest guide to the combined city museum and UNESCO-listed pagoda, with reservations, crowds, hours, and transport.

item.title: Hu Anyan’s Honesty Is More Unsettling Than an Inspirational Story
item.description: What I Deliver Parcels in Beijing asks of comfortable readers—and why respect, empathy, and solidarity matter more than pity.

item.title: What Lei Diansheng’s 10-Year Walk Can—and Cannot—Teach Us
item.description: A reflection on endurance, preparation, gendered freedom, purpose, and the danger of turning survival into a prescription.

item.title: Why Roundtable Pai Season 8 Feels So Honest
item.description: A cultural essay about sincerity, practical compromise, microhistory, and preserving ordinary lives.

item.title: Which Credit-Card Points Are Best for Business-Class Awards?
item.description: Citi, Chase, Amex, and Capital One compared by bookable airline partners, transfer ratios, fees, and real spending.

item.title: How to Book EVA Air Award Tickets for Family
item.description: A practical guide to EVA nominee registration, mileage-source restrictions, transfers, ticketing, and the no-pooling rule.

item.title: Amex Platinum Member Airfares: When the Former IAP Is Worth It
item.description: How to compare Amex’s selected premium-fare savings against booking direct, ticket rules, and agency servicing.

item.title: Citi Strata Premier + Double Cash: A Simple $95 Two-Card Setup
item.description: A current analysis of combining broad 3X categories, 2% catch-all rewards, and transferable Citi ThankYou Points.

item.title: Can You Change Your Amex Platinum Airline Choice Midyear?
item.description: The official January rule and how to request a discretionary one-time airline change before using the annual credit.

item.title: Hilton Lifetime Diamond: Requirements and How to Check Progress
item.description: The current qualification rules, what changed in 2026, and how to request official lifetime totals from Hilton.

item.title: Project Hail Mary Movie: Masterpiece or Overhyped?
item.description: A spoiler-light analysis of why the early IMDb score fell while broad audience and critic acclaim endured.

item.title: John Howie Steak Bellevue Review: Best at Happy Hour
item.description: An independent review of dinner value, happy hour, slow pacing, the service charge, and using an Amex Resy credit.

item.title: Park Hyatt Shanghai Review: Come for the View, Not the Upgrade
item.description: An independent review of the skyline rooms, service, Globalist and FHR treatment, dining, and location.

item.title: Shanghai Maglev to Pudong Airport With Luggage
item.description: A luggage-tested guide to the Longyang Road Maglev with current fares, payment, timing, and airport-buffer advice.

item.title: Taoyuan Airport Lounges: Oriental vs. Plaza Premium
item.description: A firsthand comparison of Oriental Club, Plaza Premium, and China Airlines lounges at Taipei Taoyuan Airport.

item.title: China Airlines Premium Economy: Seattle to Taipei Review
item.description: A firsthand A350 premium-economy review covering the seat, food, lounges, fare, and onward connection to Shanghai.

item.title: Sheng Yong Xing Lujiazui: Peking Duck Worth Seeking Out
item.description: An independent review of the signature duck, wok-fried cabbage, pork intestine with Laba garlic, and how to plan the meal.

item.title: Is Shanghai Astronomy Museum Worth the Trip?
item.description: An honest review with ticket, transport, planetarium, exhibit, and visitor-planning advice.

item.title: Is Waldorf Astoria Shanghai on the Bund Worth It?
item.description: An honest review of the room, breakfast, Library Lounge, Hilton Diamond treatment, and who should book.

item.title: Adora Magic City Cruise Guide: Cabins, Decks, Food and VIP
item.description: A firsthand guide to cabins, deck layout, dining, VIP benefits, and preparing to sail from Shanghai.

item.title: Jeju Cruise Port Day: Three Realistic Shore Excursions
item.description: Choose a realistic south, west, or east Jeju route, with transport options and return-to-ship advice.

item.title: Luzhou Museum: A Small Museum Worth Taking Slowly
item.description: A practical guide to Luzhou Museum and its remarkable qilin wine warmer, Han funerary art, mirrors, locks, and calligraphy.

item.title: Jinlong Temple: Look Beyond the Main Hall
item.description: A quiet Luzhou temple walk through red walls, bamboo gates, and stone steps.

item.title: How Should Non-Experts Judge Experts?
item.description: What a conversation with archaeologist Feng Shi teaches us about evidence, authority, and intellectual humility.

item.title: 13 Days Around Iceland: Ring Road, Highlands, and Westfjords
item.description: An ambitious summer road trip with realistic driving advice and room for Icelandic weather.

item.title: Project Hail Mary: Science, Friendship, and One Great Problem
item.description: Why Andy Weir's ambitious science-fiction novel works—and where its machinery shows.

item.title: The Silent Patient: Compulsive, Clever, and a Little Too Neat
item.description: A spoiler-free review of the thriller's page-turning strengths and strained final turn.

item.title: Red Rising Gets Better as It Gets Bigger
item.description: A spoiler-light review of the original trilogy and its leap from familiar premise to epic space opera.

item.title: Two Alice Feeney Thrillers for Your Next Flight
item.description: Fast, twist-heavy mysteries made for a long flight—with a few reservations.

item.title: Reading Tokyo: Why The Devotion of Suspect X Feels Uniquely Japanese
item.description: A spoiler-free review of Keigo Higashino's inverted mystery and the Japanese cultural setting that makes its emotional logic work.

item.title: The On-Device AI Revolution: What Your Phone Can Do Without the Cloud
item.description: AI that runs entirely on your phone is quietly becoming the norm. Why it matters for privacy, what it can and can't do, and how to tell which kind you're using.

item.title: AI Tools Worth Paying For (and the Free Ones That Are Enough)
item.description: AI subscriptions add up fast. A framework for deciding when a paid tier actually earns its fee — and when the free version is genuinely all you need.

item.title: How to Use AI to Plan a Trip (Without Getting Burned)
item.description: AI chatbots are genuinely good at trip planning — and confidently wrong in specific, predictable ways. What to delegate, what to always verify, and how to prompt.

item.title: The Beginner's Guide to Credit-Card Rewards
item.description: Points, miles, cash back, statement credits, and transfer partners — how the rewards system actually works, and how to get real value from the cards you already carry.

item.title: How to Track Credit-Card Benefits Before They Expire
item.description: Annual travel credits, dining credits, and free nights quietly expire unused every year. A simple system for capturing every benefit you're already paying for.

item.title: How Early Should You Arrive at the Airport?
item.description: Two hours? Three? A practical framework for airport timing — domestic vs. international, checked bags, PreCheck, peak hours, and when the standard advice breaks down.

item.title: How to Plan a Museum Visit Without Exhaustion
item.description: Museum fatigue sets in after about 90 minutes. See more by planning to see less — a curator-style approach to visits you'll actually remember.

item.title: Introducing CardPecker
item.description: A simple, private app that tells you which credit card to use for every purchase — no account required, and no data ever leaves your device.
